steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what Laurent's research suggests about investors residing in Japan. The relevant sentence states that tax havens 'are rarely used by investors living in Japan, while many investors who utilize tax havens reside in Saudi Arabia or Greece.' The answer must stay within what the text actually says — no outside inference about investment strategy, risk, or returns.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — They are engaging in investment practices that Vale would acknowledge as beneficial because they are legally permissible.: Vale disapproves of tax havens even though they are legal — she does not call them beneficial. ✗
  • Choice B — They are less likely than investors residing in Saudi Arabia to store wealth in Singapore.: Singapore is named as an example tax haven; Japan investors rarely use tax havens while Saudi Arabia investors frequently do. This is a direct restatement of the passage. ✓
  • Choice C — They are more likely than residents of Singapore to utilize investment strategies that might yield greater returns but that might also result in greater losses.: the passage says nothing about investment risk or returns. ✗
  • Choice D — They are more likely than residents of Greece to engage in tax avoidance.: the passage says the opposite — many investors in Greece use tax havens, while Japan investors rarely do. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
